title: Godpowers Project Intent (v1)
description: Hand-edited project configuration. The 'intent' artifact in the three load-bearing artifacts pattern.

apiVersion.description: Schema version. Frozen at v1.0; v2 would be breaking.

mode.description: A=greenfield, B=brownfield/gap-fill, C=audit-only, E=bluefield (greenfield-in-org-context). Mode D (multi-repo suite membership) is tracked separately by `mode-d-suite` because every repo in a suite still runs one of A/B/E underneath.

mode-d-suite.description: True when this repo is registered in a multi-repo suite. The peer agent god-coordinator owns suite-scope coordination via .godpowers/suite/.

scale.description: Drives which tiers and personas activate.

items.description: npm package with SemVer constraint, e.g. '@godpowers/security-pack@^1.0.0'

workflow.description: Name of the workflow YAML file to use.

budgets.description: Token-cost saver controls. Optional; default behavior is no budget cap, cache off.

default-max-tokens.description: Default per-agent context cap (input loadout). 80000 is a reasonable starting point on a 200K window.

model-profile.description: Bias for which model the orchestrator picks per agent. 'cheap' routes routine work (status/doctor/locate) to smaller models; high-stakes creative work always uses the standard or expensive tier.

cache.description: Enable the agent-output cache. Cache hits avoid re-spawning agents when inputs + state hash match. Opt-in; clear with /god-cache-clear.

cache-ttl-hours.description: Cache entry TTL in hours. After expiry, entries are recomputed.

agents.description: Per-agent overrides keyed by agent name (e.g. 'god-pm', 'god-architect').
